summary: Iteration 3 backend + frontend tested end-to-end. Backend: 21/21 pytest cases PASS (knowledge file-upload happy + 3 error paths, meetings create/list/cancel + requires_approval→approval row, automations catalog/create/toggle/delete + task_completed→fire chain, org/me + invite/join/role-based-403, full iteration-1+2 regression). Frontend (mobile viewport 390x844): login OK; all 5 dashboard quick tiles render & navigate (Knowledge, Integrations, Meetings, Automations, Admin); Meetings shows yellow SANDBOX banner, schedule modal, sandbox meet links match https://meet.google.com/xxx-xxxx-xxx, requires_approval=true meetings render AWAITING APPROVAL pill, cancel buttons work; Automations modal exposes trigger+action radios + Create btn; Admin renders organization name, OWNER pill, 2 team members (founder + the TEST_SecondUser created by backend join test), Generate-invite-code yields monospace 8-char chips, join field + Billing tile present; Knowledge shows 'Or upload PDF / CSV / TXT' upload button below Save. The /api/automations PATCH currently reuses IntegrationToggleIn ({enabled:bool}) — works but is a model-naming smell, see action items. SANDBOX/MOCKED Google Meet links and integrations OAuth are intentional per spec.

minor.issue: Reuses IntegrationToggleIn pydantic model for the {enabled:bool} body. Works, but model name is misleading; consider AutomationToggleIn alias.

react_native_issues.issue: Carry-over from iteration 2: 'props.pointerEvents is deprecated. Use style.pointerEvents' RN-web console warning. Low priority cosmetic.

action_items: Optional cleanup: introduce a dedicated AutomationToggleIn pydantic model instead of reusing IntegrationToggleIn for PATCH /api/automations/{aid}.
Optional: silence the persistent 'props.pointerEvents is deprecated' warning by migrating to style.pointerEvents on Pressables.
server.py is now ~1180 lines — well past the 700-line guideline. Strongly recommend splitting into routers/{auth,goals,tasks,knowledge,meetings,automations,org}.py modules.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py grew from ~850 (iter 2) to ~1180 lines in iter 3 — single-file boundary should be refactored into FastAPI routers before iter 4.
fire_automations() correctly wraps each automation in try/except so one failing automation does not block sibling triggers — good defensive design.
/api/org/join key-casing handled correctly (.upper() on both lookup and update). Invite code is 8 ascii_uppercase+digits — sufficient entropy for MVP.
DELETE /api/org/members reassigns target's organization to a personal org rather than hard-deleting the user — safe, prevents data orphaning.
Knowledge upload _extract_text caps content at 20KB before insertion — good DOS guard. 4MB raw-bytes cap enforced before decoding.
seed_data_creation: Created during test session under founder account: TEST_kickoff & TEST_sensitive_meet meetings (one cancelled, one awaiting approval), TEST_fire_on_task automation (cleaned up), TEST UI Meeting (via UI), 3 fresh invite codes, and registered TEST_SecondUser_<ts>@unnatix.dev who is now a 'member' of Test Founder's Company. Safe to leave; can be purged via Mongo if needed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: BASE_URL = https://executive-team-ai-1.preview.emergentagent.com. Seed login founder@unnatix.dev / Founder@123. Use /app/backend/tests/test_iteration3.py as the canonical iter-3 regression (21 cases, ~6s). Note that AI_EMPLOYEES has 8 entries total (ceo + 7 specialists) — assertion in test_agents corrected to 8. Google Meet links are SANDBOX (regex ^https://meet\.google\.com/[a-z]{3}-[a-z]{4}-[a-z]{3}$). /api/integrations remains SANDBOX. UI testIDs verified: dashboard-{knowledge,integrations,meetings,automations,admin}-link, meetings-{back,add}, meeting-cancel-{id}, meeting-{title-input,create-btn}, automations-{back,add,create}, automation-trigger-{key}, automation-action-{key}, admin-{back,new-invite,join-input,join-button,billing}, member-{id}, invite-{code}, knowledge-upload-button. Iteration-3 join-org flow created a real second account in founder org; if next iteration tests org-membership counts, expect >=2 members.
rca_of_issue: N/A — no production bugs found in iteration 3. Only test-side fix: initial test_agents assertion expected 9 agents but AI_EMPLOYEES catalog contains 8 (ceo + 7). Test corrected to len==8.
